content.text: The `find` command with `-name plots` is giving unexpected output, which suggests there might be a shell alias or custom function overriding the standard behavior. I'll switch to using `fd` instead, which should give cleaner results.

input.command: fd -t d 'plots' "/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS" "/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/scratch/2026-06/chime-dsa-documents-area-staging" 2>/dev/null; echo "=== acf files ==="; fd -t f -i 'acf' "/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS" "/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/scratch/2026-06/chime-dsa-documents-area-staging" 2>/dev/null | rg -i '\.(png|pdf|jpg|jpeg|svg)$'

content.text:
fd skips gitignored dirs by default — `plots/` is likely gitignored. Retrying with no-ignore.

The plots directory is probably gitignored, so I need to use the `-H` and `-I` flags with `fd` to include hidden files and ignore the gitignore rules.

input.command: fd -HI -t f -i 'acf' "/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS" "/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/scratch/2026-06/chime-dsa-documents-area-staging" "/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections" 2>/dev/null | rg -i '\.(png|pdf|jpg|jpeg|svg)$'; echo "=== any plots dirs (incl ignored) ==="; fd -HI -t d 'plots$' "/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS" 2>/dev/null

<timestamp>Monday, Jun 29, 2026, 12:57 AM (UTC-5)</timestamp>
<user_query>

can you remind me what "grand" indicates in "grand_galaxies_cgm" or "grand_clusters_icm"?
</user_query>
I need to track down where "grand" appears in the codebase and documentation, checking the FLITS repository and any Overleaf sections that might reference it.

I'm looking at the generator script to understand what "grand" refers to in the filename. Based on the captions mentioning "three dominant intervening galaxies" and "four innermost foreground clusters," it seems "grand" is highlighting the most significant systems—essentially a showcase figure of the key structures. Let me check the script's docstring to confirm this interpretation.

"grand" = the showcase composite of the **dominant/most significant** intervening systems (not all of them). Per the generator `grand_systems_figures.py`:

- `grand_galaxies_cgm` — the **three dominant intervening galaxies** whose interior/CGM source non-negligible sightline DM (1×3 composite).
- `grand_clusters_icm` — the **four innermost foreground clusters** along the FRB 20230307A field (2×2 composite).

So "grand" is the curated multi-panel "grand summary" of the headline systems, distinct from the full per-sightline budget that sums in all the lesser foreground halos.
